title: NASA USLI 2026
description: The NASA University Student Launch Initiative (USLI) is a research-based, competitive, experiential exploration project. Student teams design, build, and fly reusable rockets to achieve specific altitude and payload goals. This year's challenge includes deploying a rover and conducting soil sample analysis.

title: Spaceport America Cup 2026
description: The Spaceport America Cup is the world's largest intercollegiate rocket engineering competition. Teams from around the globe compete in various altitude categories with student-designed and built rockets. Features both solid and liquid propulsion categories with commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) and student-researched and developed (SRAD) motor options.

title: Battle of the Rockets 2026
description: A regional high-powered rocketry competition featuring multiple launch categories, technical presentations, and design reviews. Teams compete for accuracy in altitude prediction, innovative payload designs, and overall project management. Perfect for teams preparing for national competitions.

title: Team America Rocketry Challenge 2026
description: The Team America Rocketry Challenge (TARC) is the world's largest student rocket contest, with nearly 5,000 students nationwide competing each year. The challenge requires teams to design, build and launch a model rocket that meets specific altitude and flight duration targets while safely carrying a payload of raw eggs.
